Politics.headline: Trump Threatens to Deploy ICE to Airports Amid Homeland Security Shutdown
Politics.detail: Amid a partial Homeland Security shutdown, President Trump is threatening to deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports, placing 'heavy emphasis' on arresting Somali immigrants, which is causing staffing issues and delays.

Politics.headline: UK Says Iran Missile Attack on Diego Garcia Failed
Politics.detail: The United Kingdom has reported that Iran launched ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK Diego Garcia base located in the Indian Ocean, though the attack was unsuccessful.

Politics.headline: Trump Administration Launches More Probes into Harvard
Politics.detail: The Trump administration has launched two additional probes against Harvard University, alleging discrimination against students based on race, color, and national origin, as well as antisemitism on campus, following the Supreme Court's ruling against affirmative action.

Politics.headline: New Top Prosecutor Named in New Jersey, Ending Impasse Over U.S Attorney's Office
Politics.detail: Robert Frazer has been appointed as the new top prosecutor in New Jersey, replacing a trio of officials installed by the Justice Department, an arrangement a federal judge deemed illegal, thus ending the impasse over the U.S. Attorney's office.

Politics.headline: Starmer Says He Is 'Unapologetic' About His Focus on National Interest When Asked How He Deals With 'Rude' Trump
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer stated that he is 'unapologetic' about his focus on the national interest when questioned about how he manages interactions with a 'rude' Trump.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil prices ease, stock markets jump as hopes rise for end to Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: Relief is flowing through financial markets as US President Donald Trump stated that the United States has talked with Iran about a possible end to their war, leading to easing oil prices and a jump in stock prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks rebound as Trump claims "productive" U.S.-Iran talks
Finance & Economy.detail: U.S. stocks experienced solid growth following reports of potential diplomatic progress in the Middle East, which triggered a sharp decrease in oil prices and boosted investor risk appetite; however, Iranian state media denied that any negotiations had taken place.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global economy faces 'major, major threat' from Iran war, IEA head says
Finance & Economy.detail: The head of the International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that the global economy is facing a "major, major threat" due to the ongoing Iran war, stating that the crisis has had a worse impact on oil than the two oil shocks of the 1970s combined and a worse effect on gas than the Russia-Ukraine war.

Finance & Economy.headline: Israeli economy lost over $57B during 2 years of Gaza war: Central bank
Finance & Economy.detail: Israel's economy suffered losses exceeding $57 billion during two years of conflict in Gaza, which is equivalent to 8.6% of its annual gross domestic product (GDP) between 2023 and 2025, according to a report by the Bank of Israel. The losses were largely attributed to the war in Gaza, and the report does not include the economic impact of the ongoing conflict involving Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Recession risks rise for the global economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Recession risks are increasing for the global economy as the International Energy Agency (IEA) calls the current situation the greatest shock to the global energy sector, with Goldman Sachs warning that oil prices may stay above US$100 for longer due to persistent supply shocks. Iran's determination to inflict pain on global financial markets amid its conflict with the US and Israel further exacerbates these risks.

Technology.headline: Xi renews push to develop new high-tech city outside Beijing
Technology.detail: President Xi Jinping has called for Xiong'an New Area to take a leading role in tech and finance, and reaffirmed his commitment to developing a high-tech hub near Beijing, driving growth through reform and innovation and deepening links between tech and industry. The area will speed up the commercialization of research to promote emerging industries, upgrade traditional sectors, and relocate non-capital functions from Beijing.

Technology.headline: Cybersecurity trends and the expanding impact of AI: 2025-2026
Technology.detail: Cybersecurity is undergoing a profound transformation due to accelerated digital adoption and the rise of AI, which is reshaping the threat landscape by being used as both a defensive tool and an offensive weapon. Studies show that 1 in 6 breaches now involve attackers using AI, most commonly through AI-generated phishing (37%) and deepfake impersonation (35%).

Technology.headline: Microsoft Aims to Supercharge Windows Performance and Cut RAM Waste in 2026
Technology.detail: Microsoft is aiming to supercharge Windows performance and reduce RAM waste in 2026. This may help to bring back goodwill lost in 2025.

Technology.headline: Use AI to Find Your Next Rental Car With Turo's ChatGPT App
Technology.detail: Turo is using AI to help users find their next rental car with a ChatGPT app.

Technology.headline: Microsoft breaks Microsoft account sign-ins in Windows 11 with latest update
Technology.detail: Microsoft broke Microsoft account sign-ins in Windows 11 with its latest update, greeting OneDrive, Office, and Teams Free users with phantom 'no internet' errors; a restart may help if you're lucky.

Science.headline: World Meteorological Day: Ocean Heat Breaks Record, Scientists Warn Environment
Science.detail: Ocean heat has broken records, prompting scientists to issue warnings about the environment.

Science.headline: Magnetic Fluid Injected Into The Heart May Prevent Strokes, Scientists Think
Science.detail: Scientists are exploring the potential of injecting magnetic fluid into the heart as a method to prevent strokes.

Science.headline: Mysterious Structure on Mars Looks Uncannily Like an Ancient Egyptian Pyramid
Science.detail: A mysterious structure discovered on Mars bears a striking resemblance to an ancient Egyptian pyramid.

Science.headline: Bacterial Meningitis Survivors Face Life‑Altering Effects, Expert Says
Science.detail: Experts highlight the life-altering effects faced by survivors of bacterial meningitis.

Science.headline: Male bats sing in the rotor-swept zone of wind turbines, potentially raising collision risk.
Science.detail: Male bats are singing in areas near wind turbines, which could increase the risk of collisions.

Health.headline: Parkinson's patients report better balance with Brazilian capoeira dance
Health.detail: Capoeira, a martial art disguised as dance, is being used in Brazil to help patients with Parkinson's disease, with patients reporting improved physical health.

Health.headline: Weight Loss Drug Ozempic Cuts Depression, Anxiety, and Addiction Risk
Health.detail: A new study suggests that the weight loss drug Ozempic may have positive effects on mental health by reducing the risk of depression, anxiety, and addiction.

Health.headline: New Pill Cuts “bad” Cholesterol by 60% in Major Trial
Health.detail: A new pill has shown promising results in a major trial, effectively cutting bad cholesterol levels by 60%.

Health.headline: Makers Of Lyme Disease Vaccine Say It Has 70% Efficacy, Will Seek FDA's OK
Health.detail: The creators of a Lyme disease vaccine report a 70% efficacy rate and plan to seek FDA approval.

Health.headline: People 'bathe' in nature to get respite from chaotic news cycle
Health.detail: People are increasingly turning to nature for stress relief amidst a chaotic news environment.

Sports.headline: Iowa topples No. 1 and defending champion Florida in March Madness
Sports.detail: Iowa defeated Florida in a stunning upset during March Madness, ending Florida's reign as defending champion. The unranked Hawkeyes demonstrated resilience and skill to secure the victory.

Sports.headline: Tennessee makes 4th straight Sweet 16, beating Virginia in March Madness
Sports.detail: Tennessee secured their spot in the Sweet 16 for the fourth consecutive year with a win over Virginia. The team's consistent performance in the tournament highlights their strength and determination.

Sports.headline: East Bengal 7-0 Mohammedan SC: Kolkata Giants Create ISL History With Huge Win
Sports.detail: East Bengal made history in the Indian Super League (ISL) with a dominant 7-0 victory over Mohammedan SC. The Kolkata giants showcased exceptional offensive prowess in the match.

Sports.headline: Virginia becomes first First Four team to reach Sweet Sixteen after 83-75 double-OT win over Iowa
Sports.detail: Virginia made history as the first First Four team to advance to the Sweet Sixteen, following an 83-75 double-overtime victory against Iowa. The game was a hard-fought battle, showcasing Virginia's resilience and determination.

Sports.headline: Gill Reveals Secret To Calmness Ahead Of IPL 2026: "Belief And Security"
Sports.detail: Shubman Gill shared that his calmness before IPL 2026 comes from "Belief and Security". This insight provides a glimpse into his mental preparation as he approaches the tournament.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Timberlake Tells Police 'These Are Hard Tests' During DWI Arrest Body Cam Video
Entertainment.detail: Body cam footage from Justin Timberlake's 2024 DWI arrest has been released, showing him struggling with sobriety tests and telling police the tests were hard. The release comes after Timberlake previously sued to block the footage, but later reached an agreement to release a redacted version.

Entertainment.headline: Jury finds that Bill Cosby sexually assaulted woman in 1972, awards her more than $19 million
Entertainment.detail: A California civil jury has found Bill Cosby liable for drugging and sexually assaulting a woman in 1972, awarding her more than $19 million in damages. The jury found that Cosby sexually assaulted Donna Motsinger in 1972.

Entertainment.headline: Pregnant Erin Krakow and Husband Ben Rosenbaum Gush Over Becoming Parents (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Erin Krakow and Ben Rosenbaum are expecting a baby and shared their excitement about becoming parents. The couple shared exclusive details with Entertainment Tonight about preparing for their new arrival.

Entertainment.headline: Music icons named for 2026 Savannah
Entertainment.detail: Icehouse and Don McLean are the first major artists announced for the 2026 Savannah in the Round festival in Mareeba, Australia. The four-day music festival will take place from October 8-11 and feature a mix of national, international, and local performers.

Entertainment.headline: Zendaya and Tom Holland Make First Joint Appearance Amid Secret Marriage Speculation
Entertainment.detail: Zendaya and Tom Holland made their first public appearance together amidst speculation that they secretly got married. The rumors began after Zendaya's stylist, Law Roach, hinted at a secret wedding.

Crime & Justice.headline: Texas Woman Sentenced to 25 Years in Prison for Conspiring to Sexually Abuse a Child in Mexico
Crime & Justice.detail: A Texas woman was sentenced to 25 years in prison for conspiring to sexually abuse a child in Mexico. The Justice Department continues to pursue and prosecute individuals involved in child sexual abuse and exploitation.

Crime & Justice.headline: Russian Citizen Sentenced to Prison for Hacking into U.S. Companies and Enabling Major Cybercrime Groups to Extort Tens of Millions of Dollars
Crime & Justice.detail: A Russian citizen was sentenced to prison for hacking into U.S. companies and enabling major cybercrime groups to extort tens of millions of dollars. The sentencing highlights the Justice Department's commitment to combating cybercrime and holding perpetrators accountable.

Crime & Justice.headline: Orlando Man Pleads Guilty to His Role in Years-Long Off-the-Books Payroll Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: An Orlando man pleaded guilty to his role in a years-long off-the-books payroll scheme. The Justice Department is actively prosecuting individuals involved in financial crimes and tax evasion.

Crime & Justice.headline: Peers vote to back clause pardoning women convicted over illegal abortions
Crime & Justice.detail: Members of Parliament voted to support a clause that would pardon women convicted of illegal abortions. This decision reflects ongoing discussions and reforms within the UK criminal justice system related to reproductive rights.

Crime & Justice.headline: Environmental Crimes Bulletin – February 2026
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice released the Environmental Crimes Bulletin for February 2026. This bulletin likely details recent prosecutions and enforcement actions related to environmental law violations.

Education.headline: Ministry of Education halts filling student vacancies in schools under Provincial Councils
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education in Sri Lanka has suspended the filling of student vacancies in Grades 2-11 in schools operating under Provincial Councils until further notice, pending amendments to existing circulars. This decision means that schools cannot advertise vacancies or conduct interviews for student admissions until new instructions are provided, excluding admissions for Grades 1, 5, and 6 for the year 2026.

Education.headline: Philippine education department mandates simple graduation rites amid rising fuel costs
Education.detail: The Department of Education (DepEd) in the Philippines has directed all public schools nationwide to conduct modest and economical end-of-school-year ceremonies due to the recent surge in fuel prices. Education Secretary Sonny Angara emphasized that graduation rites must be conducted without excessive spending or extravagant attire, aligning with President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr.'s directive to alleviate the financial burden on Filipino families.

Education.headline: AI Integration in Education: Growth and Concerns
Education.detail: The rapid expansion of AI adoption within classrooms globally is generating both excitement and apprehension among educators and policymakers as they grapple with establishing clear guidelines and understanding the potential downsides for students. Recent headlines highlight the surge in AI adoption within classrooms globally, emphasizing the need for careful consideration of its implications.

Education.headline: Ministry of Education announces gradual resumption of in-person classes starting 24 March, full resumption by 29 March 2026
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education and Higher Education in Qatar has announced the gradual resumption of in-person learning across educational institutions, starting Tuesday, 24 March 2026. Full in-person attendance for all students in schools and kindergartens will resume on Sunday, 29 March 2026.

Education.headline: Some schools 'actively discourage' SEN support pupils, research warns
Education.detail: Research indicates that some schools may be actively discouraging students with Special Educational Needs (SEN) from seeking support. This concerning trend highlights potential barriers to inclusive education and the need for greater support for SEN pupils.

Lifestyle.headline: Cape Verde’s president launches debate on Santa Luzia
Lifestyle.detail: President José Maria Neves of Cape Verde is initiating an international discussion regarding the impact of marine pollution on Santa Luzia island, which is covered in tons of plastic and he intends to “debate the issue, bring it into the public agenda” and “mobilize international partners” at the 5th Ocean Decade Conference, to be held in July on the island of Boa Vista. The island's waste consists mostly of industrial fishing nets carried by ocean currents.

Lifestyle.headline: Mozambique hosts CPLP researchers to promote African science
Lifestyle.detail: Mozambique is currently hosting researchers from the Community of Portuguese Language Countries (CPLP) to foster the advancement of science in Africa.

Lifestyle.headline: Lea Salonga joins Les Miserables The Arena Spectacular in Singapore as Madame Thenardier
Lifestyle.detail: Tony-winning star Lea Salonga returns to the iconic musical in a new role for a limited seven-week run at Marina Bay Sands from Mar 24 to May 10.

Lifestyle.headline: Biggest Lifestyle Spring Clearance 2026
Lifestyle.detail: The Biggest Lifestyle Spring Clearance 2026 is happening now, offering savings across lifestyle categories. It's an opportunity to discover clever finds and stylish solutions.

Lifestyle.headline: Macau: residents avoid seeking psychiatric support
Lifestyle.detail: A recent report indicates that residents of Macau are hesitant to seek psychiatric support, which raises concerns about mental health awareness and access to care in the region.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Mideast war still adding pressure
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan's central bank is closely monitoring the economic impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly rising energy prices, and is prepared to take pre-emptive tightening measures if inflation expectations rise. The bank has raised its GDP growth forecast for the year but also increased inflation forecasts due to the tensions.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: No word from VP on attending impeach hearing
Opinion/Editorial.detail: It is uncertain whether Vice President Sara Duterte will attend the House hearing regarding the two impeachment complaints filed against her.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The politics of easy solutions
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discusses the dangers of advocating for policies that sound compassionate but could produce adverse economic effects, such as unemployment and inflation, and argues that wage policies should be grounded in economic evidence rather than political theater, referencing a proposed national minimum wage hike in the Philippines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Comelec's art of letting go
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) in the Philippines is criticized for clearing Sen. Rodante Marcoleta of a poll offense despite inconsistencies in his campaign finance disclosure, showcasing a talent for protecting interests other than the integrity of elections.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Another broadcaster killed, and counting
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the killing of another broadcaster and the role of the presidential task force to promote the security of media workers.

World.headline: Iran Denies Any Talks with US After Trump Claims 'Productive' Discussions
World.detail: Iran's parliament speaker stated that the US president is using the idea of talks to escape the quagmire in which the US and Israel are trapped. This comes after Trump claimed that the two countries were seeking a deal.

World.headline: Two Pilots Killed After Air Canada Jet Collision at LaGuardia in New York
World.detail: Two pilots died after an Air Canada jet collided with a vehicle at LaGuardia Airport in New York. The collision is under investigation.

World.headline: Colombian Military Plane Crash Kills at Least 34
World.detail: A Colombian military plane carrying 125 people crashed after takeoff, resulting in at least 34 fatalities. Dozens of people were rescued from the crash site.

World.headline: Tehran Calls Trump's Peace Talk Claims 'Fake News,' as Attacks Continue
World.detail: Despite claims from President Trump about productive talks, Tehran has dismissed these claims as 'fake news' amidst ongoing attacks. Iran's Revolutionary Guard has also called Trump a 'deceitful American president'.

World.headline: Security Agencies Investigate Claim Iran-Linked Group Behind London Ambulance Arson
World.detail: Security agencies in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic arson attack where ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity were set on fire. Counter-terror police are also investigating the incident.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bobi Wine slams arrest of Tendai Biti as “cowardly attack” on democracy in Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine has condemned the arrest of Zimbabwean opposition politician Tendai Biti, calling it a "cowardly attack" on democracy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Temba Mliswa outburst displays selective memory and outrage on 2008 violence
Zimbabwe.detail: An opinion piece discusses Temba Mliswa's recent outburst and suggests it highlights a selective memory regarding the violence that occurred in 2008.

Zimbabwe.headline: The opposition in all but name: How Reform UK is governing by proxy on immigration.
Zimbabwe.detail: Dr Sibangilizwe Moyo discusses how Reform UK is governing by proxy on immigration.

Zimbabwe.headline: MAN RECRUITS 35 PEOPLE, FOOLS FINANCIAL INSTITUTION INTO BELIEVING THEY ARE EMPLOYEES OF A SECURITY FIRM, STEALS US$91,5k
Zimbabwe.detail: A man recruited 35 people and deceived a financial institution into believing they were employees of a security firm, ultimately stealing US$91,500.

Zimbabwe.headline: All set for national heroes' triple burial
Zimbabwe.detail: Preparations are complete for the triple burial of national heroes.
